title: [lora_l2.c:639] lichen_lora_l2_stop documented as 'always returns 0' but can return -ECANCELED
description:
Contract: Header documentation (lora_l2.h:151) states 'Always returns 0 (stop is idempotent and cannot fail)'.
Violation: The implementation (line 715) returns -ECANCELED when the RX thread had to be forcibly aborted. This contradicts the documented contract.
Impact: Medium - Callers relying on documented behavior may not check return value. The enable() path (lichen_l2.c:1101) does check for -ECANCELED, showing the documented contract is wrong.
Mitigation: Update lora_l2.h documentation to reflect actual return values: '@return 0 on graceful stop, -ECANCELED if thread was forcibly aborted'.
close_reason: Fixed: Updated lichen_lora_l2_stop() documentation in lora_l2.h to accurately reflect return values - now documents both 0 (graceful stop) and -ECANCELED (forced abort requiring deinit/init cycle)
